title: Off topic tangent split Moe VS Eva VS character archetypes
display_title: Off topic tangent split Moe VS Eva VS character archetypes
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 6:11 pm Tankred wrote: Oh lawdy, dat Xard's on fire today!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 6:21 pm Xard wrote: She probably heard someone call pilot an elite position or something like that. This doesn't mean she had her caretakers drive in her head year after year that she's going to be part of THE elite. She's perfectly capable of forming this conception on her own, just like she seems to do in ep 22.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 6:34 pm Bagheera wrote: Sure. She was just raised in a vacuum and her upbringing had nothing whatsoever to do with her attitudes or opinions.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 6:49 pm Xard wrote: Oh look, a strawman! Never said this. Go watch ep 22 again. You don't have any proof Asuka was brainwashed into becoming a gigantic bitch. That's how she dealt with the situation she was thrust in.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:09 pm Bagheera wrote: Objection! I never said she was brainwashed. I said she wasn't raised in a vacuum. Because, well, duh!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:26 pm I find it difficult to believe that you are spending that much time trying to change another's opinion. It ain't going to work fellas. Bagheera has his point, Xard has his. If you can't deal with it, cry some more.. but alone . Is this the point in which someone calls, "topic bitches"?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:29 pm Stryker wrote: I find it difficult to believe that you are spending that much time trying to change another's opinion. It ain't going to work fellas. Bagheera has his point, Xard has his. If you can't deal with it, cry some more.. but alone . Is this the point in which someone calls, "topic bitches"?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:32 pm Been skimming this thread, and for once I pretty much agree with everything Xard's said. :3

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 23, 2011 8:16 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: Isn´t Eva a bit of a harem anime too?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Mar 23, 2011 8:24 pm Oh hey, Luc is back. Rockin'. FWIW I think it's kind of pointless weeaboo-ism to use the term moe when all "I find this thing moe" is generally used to mean is "I like this thing strongly" - you can cite all the internet scholars with their bullshit definitions of "desire for protective feelings" or "frustrated desire of the otaku for fatherhood" (!!) or whatever it is but I don't see that at all as how "moe" is used, certainly not as some kind of universal definition. The way it's used is to mean a not-necessarily-but-usually-sexual fetish. But the way it gets bandied about in most circles is just kind of silly - don't see what's so different about teenage girls on Gaia shrieking about how shit is supa kawaii desu ne~~~ XDDDD or whatever the kids are doing these days. Doesn't help that half the time when the word is brought up the discussion instantly disintegrates into THIS ISN'T WHAT MOE MEANS YOU DON'T UNDERSTAND. Fuck, I don't know. I don't say in any seriousness that I find Asuka moe because moe isn't something that I seriously think in terms of. Do I find Asuka attractive? Yes. Do I find her to be a great character? Yeah sure. Do I worship her? Yeah, in my own way, although never by herself. Do I love her? Not really, but then again I'm not sure I can define love or say that I believe that those things which love is commonly defined as exist. Do I find her moe? I can't in any seriousness say so. I don't classify my relationships with persons real or fictional as things which in me evoke moe. I think this is partly why it's confusing to use as a term - plenty of people either don't feel it, don't call it that, or don't care about it.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 23, 2011 9:25 pm MOE as a term is an otaku word that has got mainstream in Japan. So even in Japan if one is not an anime otaku then he may also misunderstand the term. However among anime fans in Japan the term does hold some meaning, and basically it is what Xard has said (give and take some). And he was right in saying that Asuka and Rei are both MOE characters : This is how anime fans in Japan would categorize them regarding moe/non-moe distinction. In anime discussion here Asuka and Rei were often quoted as the source of the "moe attributes" (moe triggers in Xard's words) and one of attribute - tsundere - became a beast of its own and many also quoted Asuka and Rei as the origin of tsundere (there were tsundere-ish characters before Eva but the archetype was not so clearly presented as such). When Eva was first aired, moe as a concept did not exist, but after all the derivative development and copying of character designs after Eva and MOE concept firmly established, people looked back at Eva and found the characters were where all the explosion of moe designs rooted -- Asuka and Rei may not be like the moe characters we now see, but they are the ancestors (or queens as some put it) of moe characters. In summary MOE is a real word with meaning, not an overgeneralized term (at least not yet) in contemporary Japanese usage. How it is being used (and twisted or distorted) in western fandom usage is a separate matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:24 pm symbv wrote: MOE as a term is an otaku word that has got mainstream in Japan. So even in Japan if one is not an anime otaku then he may also misunderstand the term. However among anime fans in Japan the term does hold some meaning, and basically it is what Xard has said (give and take some). And he was right in saying that Asuka and Rei are both MOE characters : This is how anime fans in Japan would categorize them regarding moe/non-moe distinction. In anime discussion here Asuka and Rei were often quoted as the source of the "moe attributes" (moe triggers in Xard's words) and one of attribute - tsundere - became a beast of its own and many also quoted Asuka and Rei as the origin of tsundere (there were tsundere-ish characters before Eva but the archetype was not so clearly presented as such). When Eva was first aired, moe as a concept did not exist, but after all the derivative development and copying of character designs after Eva and MOE concept firmly established, people looked back at Eva and found the characters were where all the explosion of moe designs rooted -- Asuka and Rei may not be like the moe characters we now see, but they are the ancestors (or queens as some put it) of moe characters.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Mar 23, 2011 10:42 pm Bagheera wrote: I think that's the problem I have with it. As with tsundere they gave rise to moe even though they don't seem to fit with the archetypes now associated with it (Asuka's very tsun tsun and very little dere dere, at least toward Shinji; I guess it works a little better if you factor in her attitude toward Kaji. Rei, by contrast, is almost all dere dere, though of course if you take her and Asuka as a unit I guess it works there as well).

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 24, 2011 12:53 am i love for it for the sake of it being eva, and trying to still remain sane by trying to understand it more and more day by day, from different perspectives and through different reasonings. also the music :3

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 24, 2011 2:02 am symbv wrote: there is an introvert cold tsundere Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Mar 24, 2011 2:53 am Mr. Tines wrote: The term kuudere gets bandied about for the style of moe that Rei expresses. What the kuu part originally meant I've not been able to pick up from context.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Mar 24, 2011 3:05 am Mr. Tines wrote: What the kuu part originally meant I've not been able to pick up from context.

[]:             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Thu Mar 24, 2011 4:15 am Generally speaking, I think Xard's insights into anime are very remarkable. The only thing I would say is that, if it is true that Eva is ultimately responsible for what people think of as moe , then it may be that there is eventually a limit to understanding Eva in terms of moe . I do think Eva really produced a trauma in otaku culture. I think Anno's intent was go as far as possible down the path which had been blazed by Yamato and the original Gundam (as well as Ideon and some others). I think Yamato is probably the beginning of a cycle which concludes with EoE. I suspect that, by the time of EoE, or even before that time, Anno concluded or feared that the spirit embodied in such works could only produce, in the last analysis, something like Aum, and had to be destroyed or criticized. I think, if Anno had not undertaken something at a very deep level, Eva would not have produced such trauma. In this sense, I wonder if the reworking of Eva into "moe," as well as the shift in the core of otaku culture from "idealistic" sci-fi elements to "escapist" eroge -like elements, was a response to this trauma (as Azuma and some others intimate). Of course both are present before and after Eva, but the relative importance of the two is reversed. One small note on Rebuild. Azuma writes (this is from p.182 of Mechademia 2): Anno flirted with the impossible task of constructing a grand narrative in the 1990s, but in the end it proved impossible, and all that remained was Ayanami Rei as a moe kyara, that is, as an affective figure. In this respect, I think that the scene in the twenty-sixth episode of Evangelion in which Ayanami Rei appears running with bread in her mouth marks a turning point in otaku culture, the moment when the Era of Fictions became the Era of Animals, when the Era of Fictional Histories gave way to the Era of Affective Response to Characters (kyara moe). This is why Evangelion remains such an important work.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Mar 24, 2011 4:20 am Azathoth wrote: "kuuru", I think.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 24, 2011 5:56 am symbv wrote: A common development of tsundere character is that later she would become more welcoming and receptive towards the male character. This is what happened with Rei in Eva. Some would also say Asuka getting more receptive to Shinji later in the show (but before she broke down)
